content.problemStatement:
A number MMM is said to be a Mystical Number with respect to a number XXX if (M⊕X)>(M&X)(M \oplus X) \gt (M \& X)(M⊕X)>(M&X).

You are given an array AAA of size NNN. You are also given QQQ queries. Each query consists of three integers LLL, RRR, and XXX.

For each query, find the count of Mystical Numbers in the subarray A[L:R]A[L:R]A[L:R] with respect to the number XXX.

**Notes:**


⊕\oplus⊕ represents the Bitwise XOR operation and &\&& represents the Bitwise AND operation.
A[L:R]A[L:R]A[L:R] denotes the subarray A[L],A[L+1],…,A[R]A[L], A[L+1], \ldots, A[R]A[L],A[L+1],…,A[R].

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.
The third line of each test case contains an integer QQQ - denoting the number of queries.
The ithi^{th}ith of the next QQQ lines contains three space-separated integers LLL , RRR and XXX.


Output Format
For each testcase,



For each query, print in a new line, the count of Mystical Numbers among A[L],A[L+1],…,A[R]A[L], A[L+1], \ldots, A[R]A[L],A[L+1],…,A[R] with respect to the number XXX.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
Sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

A5⊕X=1,A5&X=4A_5 \oplus X = 1, A_5 \& X = 4A5​⊕X=1,A5​&X=4.

A3⊕X=1,A3&X=2A_3 \oplus X = 1, A_3 \& X = 2A3​⊕X=1,A3​&X=2.

A5⊕X=7,A5&X=0A_5 \oplus X = 7, A_5 \& X = 0A5​⊕X=7,A5​&X=0.
